Title: Chromatin-mediated feed-forward auxin biosynthesis in floral meristem determinacy

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant hormone controlling floral meristem termination and gynoecium formation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: terminating floral meristem and developing carpels
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of TRN2 partially rescues the crc knu indeterminate floral meristem phenotype, indicating TRN2 negatively affects proper auxin homeostasis during floral meristem termination
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TRN2 is a direct downstream target of CRC reported and tested in this study context. 2. trn2 mutation partially rescued crc knu floral meristem indeterminacy and reduced WUS expression. 3. Simultaneous TRN2 removal and YUC4 misexpression strongly rescued crc knu, showing cooperative parallel action with YUC4 in auxin homeostasis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genetic mutant analysis; combinatorial genetics with crc-1 knu-1 trn2-1; qRT-PCR of WUS; fruit morphology and histology
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: standard growth at 22 °C under continuous light
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pCRC promoter used to compare combinatorial rescue with YUC4 misexpression
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: trn2 single mutant fruits are twisted
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TRN2 is not an enzyme or TF; categorized here as Common Gene. In crc knu background, removal of TRN2 and ectopic YUC4 expression had additive rescue effects, supporting parallel CRC downstream branches.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream step of the two-step TAA/YUC pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes the YUCCA-dependent oxidative conversion in the major auxin biosynthesis pathway, producing IAA from the TAA-derived indole-3-pyruvate route
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: critical step in the major pathway synthesizing bioactive auxin; key control point implied for local auxin production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant hormone directing floral meristem termination, gynoecium formation, and associated cell wall remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: abaxial side of epidermal cells in stage 6 carpels / terminating floral meristem
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: YUC4 activation promotes local auxin accumulation and partially rescues crc-dependent floral meristem indeterminacy and cell wall defects; together with TRN2 modulation it strongly restores determinacy
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. YUC4 was identified as a common direct target of AG and CRC. 2. YUC4 transcript levels decrease in ag, crc, ag crc, and chr11 chr17 mutants, and increase upon AG-GR or CRC-GR induction. 3. CRC directly binds conserved YABBY-binding motifs in the YUC4 promoter, and CRM4 contributes to stage-specific expression. 4. IAA levels are reduced in crc mutants consistent with reduced YUC4 expression. 5. pCRC::YUC4 partially rescues crc and crc knu fruit and determinacy defects, reduces ectopic WUS expression, and restores galactan/xyloglucan patterns. 6. Combined YUC4 activation and trn2 loss strongly rescue crc knu.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transcriptome reanalysis; qRT-PCR; pYUC4::GUS reporter; in situ hybridization; AG-GR and CRC-GR induction; promoter deletion/editing; ChIP-qPCR for CRC and CHR11; FAIRE; Pol II ChIP; IAA LC-MS/MS quantification; transgenic rescue with pCRC::YUC4; phenotyping; WUS expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: highest in abaxial side of epidermal cells in carpels at stage 6; restricted to carpel tip at stage 7; also expressed in sepals but sepal expression is not AG/CRC regulated
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native YUC4 promoter (3.7 kb); mutated YUC4 promoter with disrupted YABBY-binding sites; pCRC promoter for ectopic YUC4 expression; 35S and genomic CRC/AG-GR systems for upstream induction experiments
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: dexamethasone induction of AG-GR or CRC-GR; floral stage-specific analyses; continuous light growth
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: YUC4 activation is linked to chromatin opening. AG with CHR11/CHR17 increases chromatin accessibility at the YUC4 promoter; ag and chr11 chr17 reduce FAIRE accessibility and Pol II occupancy. Discussion suggests this feed-forward loop may overcome Polycomb-mediated silencing.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: reanalysis of four published transcriptome datasets; public AG ChIP-seq dataset; published DNase I hypersensitivity data; published MNase-seq/nucleosome occupancy data (accessions not given in text)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: YUC4 alone provides partial rescue, implying redundancy with other YUC/auxin regulators
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Deletion of CRM4 partially reduces YUC4 expression, indicating additional regulatory regions. No flower-specific DNase I hypersensitive YABBY-binding region was found in close homologs YUC1, YUC2, and YUC6. YUC4 misexpression restored cell wall galactan and xyloglucan defects in crc knu.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: direct transcriptional activation of YUC4; chromatin opening through association with CHR11/CHR17; feed-forward regulation with CRC
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: auxin biosynthesis during floral meristem termination
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates YUC4 directly; activates CRC directly; indirectly contributes to WUS repression through auxin pathway
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: positive feed-forward loop with CRC on YUC4
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant hormone controlling floral meristem termination and gynoecium formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AG promotes local auxin biosynthesis by activating YUC4; ag mutation reduces YUC4 expression, chromatin accessibility, and Pol II occupancy at YUC4
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AG was identified as a direct upstream regulator of YUC4 among genes co-regulated with CRC. 2. ag mutation decreases YUC4 transcript accumulation and YUC4 promoter activity in stage 6 carpels. 3. Short-term AG-GR induction increases YUC4 expression. 4. ag mutants show reduced chromatin accessibility and Pol II occupancy at YUC4. 5. Simultaneous AG and CRC activation gives stronger YUC4 induction than either alone.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transcriptome reanalysis; public AG ChIP-seq integration; qRT-PCR in ag mutants; pYUC4::GUS in ag background; dex-inducible AG-GR assay; FAIRE; Pol II ChIP
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: AG is linked to chromatin opening at YUC4 via CHR11/CHR17 chromatin remodelers; proposed to overcome Polycomb-mediated silencing
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: AG-CHR11/CHR17 chromatin remodeling complex proposed/supported; no direct AG-CRC protein interaction detected by yeast two-hybrid
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed during floral meristem stages prior to and during termination; overlaps with CHR11/CHR17 during flower development
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AG is the master regulator of floral meristem termination in this system, but in this paper its metabolism-relevant core role is the direct activation of the auxin biosynthesis gene YUC4.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating AG-regulated auxin biosynthesis could tune reproductive organ determinacy, but broad developmental effects of AG likely limit direct engineering use.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong developmental pleiotropy expected because AG is a master floral regulator
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: dexamethasone induction; floral developmental stage specificity
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: reanalysis of transcriptomes; public AG ChIP-seq; published chromatin accessibility/nucleosome datasets
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: direct upstream activator that enables chromatin-mediated activation of YUC4 and local auxin production at the correct developmental stage

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: direct transcriptional activation of YUC4 via conserved YABBY-binding sites; repression of TRN2 reported in prior work and integrated here
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: auxin biosynthesis and auxin homeostasis during floral meristem termination
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: direct target of AG; stage 6 reproductive developmental program
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: participates with AG in a positive feed-forward loop activating YUC4
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant hormone controlling floral meristem termination, gynoecium formation, and cell wall remodeling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CRC promotes local auxin accumulation by direct activation of YUC4 and contributes to floral meristem determinacy; crc mutants show reduced YUC4 expression and lower IAA levels
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRC expression overlaps spatially with YUC4 in abaxial carpels at stage 6. 2. crc mutation lowers YUC4 transcript levels and promoter activity. 3. CRC-GR induction rapidly activates YUC4. 4. CRC directly binds the YUC4 promoter CRM4 region containing conserved YABBY-binding sites, and mutation of these sites reduces reporter expression. 5. crc mutants contain reduced IAA. 6. Two direct CRC targets, YUC4 and TRN2, cooperatively control floral meristem termination and WUS repression.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in crc mutants; pYUC4::GUS reporter in crc backgrounds; dex-inducible CRC-GR assay; CRC-myc ChIP-qPCR; promoter mutagenesis of YABBY-binding sites; IAA LC-MS/MS; combinatorial genetics with trn2 and pCRC::YUC4
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: CRC itself did not significantly alter YUC4 chromatin accessibility alone by FAIRE, but acts after AG-mediated chromatin opening and may further promote transcriptional activation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: gCRC-GR; native YUC4 promoter and mutated promoter reporters; pCRC::YUC4 rescue construct
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No direct CRC-AG protein interaction detected by yeast two-hybrid
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in abaxial carpels beginning at stage 6; overlaps with YUC4 expression domain
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CRC is decisive for stage- and tissue-specific local auxin regulation in gynoecium formation. It affects YUC4 transcription through conserved cis-elements in CRM4 and acts in parallel with TRN2-mediated auxin homeostasis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Tissue-specific CRC-driven auxin engineering may allow local modulation of reproductive organ determinacy with fewer whole-plant effects than global auxin manipulation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: crc mutants show shorter wider fruits, unfused fruit tips, and shorter styles; altered CRC activity likely causes reproductive organ morphology defects
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: reanalysis of transcriptomes; promoter conservation analysis; public AG ChIP-seq and DNase hypersensitivity resources
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: direct tissue-specific activator of YUC4 and regulator of TRN2, making CRC a key controller of local auxin homeostasis in terminating floral meristems

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: chromatin opening / nucleosome remodeling enabling YUC4 transcription
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: auxin biosynthesis gene accessibility and activation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: acts as AG-associated chromatin remodeling factor during floral meristem termination
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promotes open chromatin and Pol II occupancy at YUC4, supporting expression
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: supports AG-mediated feed-forward activation of YUC4
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant hormone for floral meristem termination and gynoecium formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CHR11 is required, together with CHR17, for proper YUC4 expression and chromatin accessibility, thereby promoting local auxin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHR11 is expressed in stage 6 floral buds. 2. YUC4 expression is reduced in chr11 chr17 double mutants. 3. CHR11 binds the proximal YUC4 promoter similarly to AG. 4. chr11 chr17 mutants show reduced YUC4 chromatin accessibility and reduced Pol II occupancy.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter GUS expression; qRT-PCR in chr11-1 chr17-1; pYUC4::GUS in mutant; GFP-CHR11 ChIP-qPCR; FAIRE; Pol II ChIP
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: direct chromatin remodeling factor; increases chromatin accessibility at YUC4 locus
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: AG-CHR11/CHR17 chromatin remodeling complex proposed and supported by prior proteomics plus current functional data
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: stage 6 floral buds / terminating floral meristems
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional conclusions were mainly from the chr11 chr17 double mutant; single-gene contribution was not separated here.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Chromatin-level tuning of auxin biosynthesis genes could provide developmental-stage-specific regulation, but broad chromatin remodeler effects may limit practical deployment.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: chr11 chr17 mutants display split-style/carpel fusion defects; exogenous auxin treatment did not rescue under tested conditions
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developmental stage-specific analyses; exogenous NAA treatment tested for mutant rescue
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: published MNase-seq/nucleosome occupancy data integrated; no accession provided in text
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: acts as chromatin accessibility factor required for AG-dependent activation of YUC4

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: chromatin opening / nucleosome remodeling enabling YUC4 transcription
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: auxin biosynthesis gene accessibility and activation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: acts with CHR11 as AG-associated chromatin remodeling factor
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promotes YUC4 expression indirectly through increased accessibility in chr11 chr17 context
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: supports AG-mediated feed-forward activation of YUC4
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant hormone for floral meristem termination and gynoecium formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CHR17 acts redundantly with CHR11 to support YUC4 transcription and auxin-related floral determinacy
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHR17 is expressed in stage 6 floral buds. 2. Loss of CHR11 and CHR17 together reduces YUC4 expression, YUC4 promoter activity, chromatin accessibility, and Pol II occupancy. 3. CHR17 therefore contributes to chromatin-mediated activation of auxin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter GUS expression; qRT-PCR in chr11-1 chr17-1; pYUC4::GUS in mutant; FAIRE; Pol II ChIP
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: direct chromatin remodeling factor acting redundantly with CHR11
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: stage 6 floral buds / terminating floral meristems
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific binding of CHR17 to YUC4 was inferred from double-mutant behavior and prior partnership with CHR11; direct CHR17 ChIP was not shown here.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: As with CHR11, chromatin engineering potential exists but likely carries high pleiotropic risk.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: broad developmental defects expected from chromatin remodeler perturbation
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: published MNase-seq/nucleosome occupancy data integrated; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: redundant chromatin remodeler needed for full activation of YUC4 and local auxin biosynthesis
